hypothesis: If a Transformer-based encoder–decoder is trained with a joint spectrum-to-spectrum reconstruction loss (L_reconstruction) in addition to the standard sequence-to-sequence loss (L_S2S) and multitask learning across multiple concentration ratios, then it will accurately identify and reconstruct the constituent pure-component spectra from IR spectra of mixtures (binary and ternary across 10%–90% ratios) with higher accuracy and spectral similarity than a model trained without the reconstruction loss, because the reconstruction objective aligns encoder representations with pure spectra and improves disambiguation of overlapping spectral features.
